free_text: Proton and deuteron production in neutron-induced
reactions on carbon at En=42.5, 62.7, and 72.8 MeV
Slypen, Meulders
Chadwick
Corcalciuc
Full cross section tables
are given,  data for H-1 and H-2 emission
J.P.Meulders - DDX graphs
Prelim.results in graphs at 62.7MeV
The experimental set-up described in details.
Experimental details
Analysis details
Triton and
helium production on carbon at the same energies
- thick target
correction procedure
- final data
Louvain-la-Neuve cyclotron 'CYCLONE'
10 microampers proton current beam
*EN-RSL - half width of the incident neuron energy bin
Four charged-particles detector
telescopes. Each consists of delta-E plastic scintil-
lator NE102 and E-detector CsI(Tl)
* The collimation system of each charged particle
detector telescope had an angular opening between 2.
and 3. degr.
Time-of-flight method for N-energy determination
E-deltaE  method for particles identification
.Elemental carbon target 5*5 cm2 surface and 1 mm
thickness
.Corrections were made on the sample thickness for
low energy charged particles
Total error includes:
The overall relative errors of the
points in the spectra are about 5%, except for
low-energy parts where they can be larger as a result
of the thick target correction procedure.
The uncertainty in the cross-section
absolute scale is less than 6% includes following:
The uncertainty in reference (n,p) cr. sect.
The uncertainty in  beam monitoring
The uncertainty in statistics in the H(n,p)
recoil proton peak
The uncertainty in solid-angle corrections
Uncertainty in number of target nuclei
The estimated experimental errors indicated in
integr. cr. sect. take in to count a 10% error for the
theoretical cross sections used to extrapolate below
the experimental energy cutoff.
By S.Benck (2015-05-05): The 42.5 MeV data were not
retained in the final analysis as it did not permit
to profit from its low energy tail in the incident
neutron energy spectrum.
The measured dataset that has been published in
I.Slypen et al.,At.Data Nucl.Data Tables 76(2000)26,
(see 22718 Entry) can effectively be considered as the
final version of carbon related data. The threshold
energies has been uniformly defined during the release
of this data set for all measured spectra at the
various angles.
Data are received from I.Slypen
+ +  Compiled by S.M.     + +
Date is corrected. Entry restored from the
old data set and corrected
Last corrections have been done.
SD: ref. on conf.  94GATLIN was added,
SF8=EXP in Reaction code deleted in Subents:002-013
REL-REF is added in 003-005, 011-013 Subents
SD: COMMENT from S.Benck was added.
STATUS "superseded" added to Subents 2-17. Calculated
data lines (marked by FLAG) were moved to separated
REACTION (multiple reaction formalism used) in Subents
002-005, 010-013. REACTION corrected in Subents 006-009
and 014-017. Ref. on PR/C,53,1309,1996 moved to REL-REF
Several Subents are moved to 22704 Entry (only proton
and deuteron production are kept in PR/C,51,1303).
ERR-ANALYS update.
SD: Small correction in ERR-ANALYS.
Data calculated with an intranuclear cascade code,
including alpha clustering and particle pickup,
followed by a Fermi-breakup mechanism, incorporating
decay via intermediate particle-unstable states.
(see REL-REF)
for En = 62.7 MeV (by S.Benck)
for En = 72.8 MeV (by S.Benck)
Data for En=42.5 MeV were not used in final analysis
(see COMMENT in Subent 1).

before) were moved to separated DATA col. (see
REACTION (2)). EN-RSL-FW -> EN-RSL moved to DATA
section. E-RSL, ANG-RSL deleted (wrong codes were used)
